title: The Desolator
description: Endure convoluted steps in order to craft a powerful nuclear bomb made out of suspicious substances!
body:
# The excitement of bombing things (in Minecraft) has just gotten a lot better!

**With the Desolator, you can clear a massive sphere-like area in a matter of (laggy) seconds. ItsJim fans rejoice!**

**In order to create the desolator, you will first need to complete a few steps, and hopefully not die of radiation in the process.**

**This mod has custom advancements to guide you, but regardless, the features will still be mentioned below!**

ㅤ 

## Structures:


![Mushroom Investigation Structure Image](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/PrIpjCdp/images/ab911a43354eeead7380550980e9b747ae04ac6d.png)

- This is the Mushroom Investigation structure, which only spawns in Mushroom Fields biomes.
- It functions as a nice little rabbit farm, however inside lies a slightly average story you can discover.

ㅤ 

## Blocks and Items:

![Radioactive Stone and Deepslate Images](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/e741868cf04f296ff3e39df35299f6aed017d452.png)

- After the desolator explodes, Stone and Deepslate will become irradiated.
- Being around radioactive blocks like these will irradiate you.

ㅤ 

![Suspicious Goop and Layer Images](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/74dfb32de6df13cf3251c7c1761acbdb8e6b7132.png)

- After the desolator explodes, it will leave behind waste in the form of Suspicious Goop blocks and layers.
- These blocks are also radioactive.

ㅤ 

![Desolation Immunity Potion](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/1677175bd29604621746b8dfe8843ff51d487f04.png)

- Brewing Sticky Goop into an Awkward Potion will give you a new potion, the Potion of Desolation Immunity, which lasts for 5 minutes.
- Check the status effects section to see what the effect itself does.



ㅤ 

![Nuclear Waste Barrel Image](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/b23638316bf0f340cb1240d8e8cdaf4d4b7480d5.png)

- The Nuclear Waste Barrel is an item that will appear whenever you attempt to fish on a Mushroom Island.
- It is considered a radioactive item, so having it in your inventory will irradiate you.

ㅤ 

![Rabbit's Foot Bits Image](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/36bbb62ecb83531b06ee008c5e4d81aa476bfe23.png)

- The Rabbit's Foot Bits can be made by stonecutting a Rabbit's Foot.
- You can eat this item, but it's not very good for you.

ㅤ 

![Gutted Milk Bucket Image](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/84a83e496e53bc042f37d34c9358ec4a24ec5b51.png)

- The Gutted Milk Bucket is made by brewing a Milk Bucket with some Rabbit's Foot Bits.
- This will clear your effects like normal milk and leave you with some other pretty nasty ones.

![Recipe for the Gutted Milk Bucket](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/ff784f674f13854f5fe115aeabec1fe41913673d.png)

ㅤㅤ 

![Radioactive Gutted Milk Bucket Image](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/6a2870184e532f66ba4dcba92e6599ff196fad20.png)

- The Radioactive Gutted Milk Bucket is an added onto variation of the Gutted Milk Bucket.
- It is also a Radioactive item, but drinking it will have the added debuff of Radiation Poisoning.
- It can be crafted with 1 Gutted Milk Bucket, Nuclear Waste Barrel, Acacia Log, and Jungle Log.

![Crafting recipe for Radioactive Gutted Milk Bucket](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/390a83785a264c7a1d03d9486b6cd4682f7e94cb.png)

ㅤ 
ㅤ 

![Sticky Goop Image](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/7061fa06244c5ac45d7bfe3a69f536cfc0c7c5a1.png)

- The Sticky Goop is produced whenever you drink a Radioactive Gutted Milk Bucket.
- Surprisingly, this goop is not made out of any radioactive material, so you're fine to carry this around all you'd like!

ㅤ 

![Fissile Cylinder](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/ee89d8816924b356e63ea2cc8fc08171762b0825.png)

- The Fissile Cylinder is a vital item within the creation of nuclear tnt components.
- Made by irradiating a ball of iron in the cylinder.

![Recipe for Fissile Cylinder](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/a97e4d15cd07aab7bd8ceee0cf66f3c656a365a0.png)

ㅤ 

![Nuclear TNT Components Image](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/c85254142c4b9987b2e14292ca6d270983e52524.png)

- The Nuclear TNT Components is a vital item within the creation of the desolator.
- It can be crafted with a Yellow Candle, Copper Ingot, Black Candle, Iron Bars, TNT Minecart, Redstone Dust, Amethyst Shard, Fissile Cylinder, and Blaze Rod.

![Nuclear TNT Components Recipe](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/d606e9bfb7297d66afc6fc88dc4f789d03c59bc4.png)

ㅤ 
ㅤ 

![THE DESOLATOR IMAGE!](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/b2660a3de9a067b4f0cf9b42274b56845f23bd9b.png)

- The star of the mod, the Desolator!
- It will light and eventually detonate whenever a Fire Charge or Flint and Steel is used on it, or when it recieves redstone power.
- Using Shears on the lit desolator will disable it and it will return to you as an item.
- The desolator will decimate a sphere-like area of around 250 blocks wide and 100 blocks tall.
- A large amount of the crater will be filled with Magma Blocks on fire with Netherrack surrounding it, along with the Suspicious Goop blocks.
Suspicious Goop Layers will also be spread out a lot further than the blast itself.
- It can be crafted with 2 Redstone Dust, 1 Piston, 2 Nuclear TNT Compnents, 1 Sticky Goop, 2 Iron Blocks, and 1 Netherite Ingot.

![Desolator Recipe](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/538cc32c5d60f86c271ec28e3353aa4737cc849a.png)


ㅤ 



## Status Effects:


![Irradiated Effect Icon](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/96c75c9671a3ea81e3eb4e6c818a01a6d3e7da8c.png)

- Irradiated renders a green noisy vignette overlay on your screen, increases fog, and makes the sky a look a little green. It also poisons you whenever you take damage.
- The effect can also transfer to entities you attack or even to entities that attack you.
- You still won't be safe if you decide to seclude yourself, as you will also take damage once every 6 seconds, kicking off the poison again.

ㅤ 

![Radiation Poisoning effect icon](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/1dc7660064d04f7efffe36c712729de7755ce4c9.png)

- Radiation Poisoning is similar to Irradiated, but is used in a more infected kind of manner.
- The only ways to get this effect are by drinking a Radioactive Gutted Milk Bucket, since you literally drank radioactivity it will infect you, and to be in the range of a detonating desolator.
- This effect is far deadlier than Irradiated, as every time 3 seconds pass, the timer will pause for a random amount of time to rapidly damage you, ignoring invulnerability frames.

ㅤ 

![Desolation Immunity Icon](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/07ecc219b66b127e07a5ec7efb13361aa5ede642.png)

- Desolation Immunity prevents you from being damaged and poisoned by the Irradiated and Radiation Poisoning effects.
- It also prevents the overlay from showing up, however the sky will still be a little greener.
- This does not make you immune to a desolator explosion. "The radiation is one thing but THE BLAST.. INCINERATES EVERYTHING!" - Jude MWS because it was funny
- You can get this effect from a Potion of Desolation Immunity, mentioned above in the blocks and items section.

ㅤ 


## Tags: (If you wanted to know what they did)



```
desolator_purpleshiny:radioactive_blocks
```
- Block tag that determines radioactive blocks, which will give you the Irradiated effect when you are close to them.

ㅤ 

```
desolator_purpleshiny:unbreakable
```
- Block tag that determines what blocks a desolator cannot remove, like bedrock.

ㅤ 

```
desolator_purpleshiny:ungoopable
```
- Block tag that determines what blocks a desolator cannot replace with goop or magma.
- The unbreakable tag is also included within this tag.

ㅤ 

```
desolator_purpleshiny:irradiation_items
```
- Item tag that determines what will give you the Irradiated effect when it's in your inventory.

ㅤ 

```
desolator_purpleshiny:lights_desolator_consume
```
- Item tag that determines what can light a desolator, with the item being consumed in the process.
- Ex: Fire Charge

ㅤ 

```
desolator_purpleshiny:lights_desolator_damage
```
- Item tag that determines what can light a desolator, with the item instead being damaged in the process.
- Ex: Flint and Steel

ㅤ 

```
desolator_purpleshiny:lights_desolator_noitemdamage
```
- Item tag that determines what can light a desolator, with the item not changing whatsoever in the process.
- (Empty by default but you can add whatever you want)

ㅤ 

```
desolator_purpleshiny:disables_desolator_consume
```
- Item tag that determines what can disable a lit desolator, with the item being consumed in the process.
- (Empty by default but you can add whatever you want)

ㅤ 

```
desolator_purpleshiny:disables_desolator_damage
```
- Item tag that determines what can disable a lit desolator, with the item instead being damaged in the process.
- Ex: Shears

ㅤ 

```
desolator_purpleshiny:disables_desolator_noitemdamage
```
- Item tag that determines what can disable a lit desolator, with the item not changing whatsoever in the process.
- (Empty by default but you can add whatever you want)


ㅤ 

```
desolator_purpleshiny:armor_prevents_radiation_from_blocks_and_items
```
- Item tag that determines armor pieces that should prevent you from getting the Irradiated effect while being around radioactive blocks, having radioactive items in your inventory, and being around a radioactive biome (empty biome tag by default).
- This does not prevent damage if you are somehow irradiated, because that's how hazmat suits work. They just prevent breaches, not make you immune.
- (Only if all 4 of your armor slots contain items in this tag)
- (This mod does not feature a hazmat suit, however you can add your own, using this item tag, if you want.)
- Ex: Hazmat armor from Alex's Caves


ㅤ 

```
desolator_purpleshiny:radiation_blast_immune
```
- Entity tag that determines what entities are immune to the blast of a desolator.

ㅤ 

```
desolator_purpleshiny:radiation_immune
```
- Entity tag that determines what entities are immune to the radiation effects.

ㅤ 

```
desolator_purpleshiny:irradiation_biomes_addon
```
- Biome tag that determines if a biome will give you the Irradiated effect.
- Doesn't irradiate you under Y=50.
- (This mod does not feature radioactive biomes, however you can add your own, using this biome tag, if you want.)

ㅤ
